description: Migrate video translation pipeline from Replicate APIs to self-hosted RunPod Serverless with Qwen3-32B LLM translation

userStories.title: Archive cloud-based translation code
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to archive the Replicate-based cloud translation code so that the codebase clearly reflects the new self-hosted architecture.
acceptanceCriteria: cloud_translate.py moved to archives/cloud_translate.py
requirements-cloud.txt moved to archives/requirements-cloud.txt
archives/ directory has README explaining archived code
Main requirements.txt updated to remove Replicate/OpenAI deps
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in iteration 1. Files archived, requirements updated, syntax checks pass.

userStories.title: Create translate_video function interface
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a clean function interface in translate.py that can be called by the RunPod handler, returning structured results instead of using Rich CLI output.
acceptanceCriteria: New translate_video(video_url, target_lang, output_dir, progress_callback=None) function in translate.py
Returns dict with: output_url, duration, segments_count, speakers_count
Progress callback receives: stage, progress_pct, detail for status updates
Uses config.py settings: WHISPER_SAMPLE_RATE=16000, CHATTERBOX_SAMPLE_RATE=24000
Voice samples extracted at 24kHz mono per config (VOICE_SAMPLE_DURATION=10s)
Audio mixing uses BACKGROUND_VOLUME=1.0 (full original volume for better UX)
Existing CLI main() function calls new translate_video() internally
Works without Rich console when progress_callback is provided
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in iteration 2. translate_video() API added with TypedDict types, progress callback support, config integration.

userStories.title: Integrate Qwen3-32B for translation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to replace Google Translate with Qwen3-32B LLM for higher-quality context-aware translations.
acceptanceCriteria: New llm_translate.py module with translate_segments_llm() function
Uses transformers library to load Qwen3-32B model (4-bit quantized for VRAM efficiency)
Batches segments per config: LLM_MAX_SEGMENTS_PER_BATCH=50, LLM_BATCH_OVERLAP=5
Uses LLM_MAX_TOKENS=4096 and LLM_TEMPERATURE=0.3 from config
Graceful fallback to Google Translate if LLM fails
Model loaded once and reused across segments
translate.py imports and uses new LLM translation
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in iteration 3. Created llm_translate.py with Qwen3-32B 4-bit quantized, batch processing, fallback to Google Translate.

userStories.title: Create RunPod handler
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a RunPod serverless handler that wraps translate_video() and handles job input/output.
acceptanceCriteria: runpod/handler.py created with handler(job) function
Accepts input: video_url, target_lang, voice_sample_url (optional)
Returns output: status, output_url, duration, error (if failed)
Uploads result to Supabase Storage and returns signed URL
Reports progress via RunPod job refresh mechanism
Handles errors gracefully with meaningful error messages
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in iteration 4. Created runpod/handler.py with full job handling, input validation, Supabase upload, signed URL generation, progress reporting.

userStories.title: Create RunPod Dockerfile
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Dockerfile that packages all ML models and dependencies for RunPod deployment.
acceptanceCriteria: runpod/Dockerfile created with CUDA 12.4 base image
ffmpeg installed via apt
All Python dependencies installed from requirements.txt
Whisper medium model pre-downloaded and baked into image
Demucs htdemucs model pre-downloaded and baked into image
Container runs handler.py as entrypoint
Image builds successfully: docker build -f runpod/Dockerfile -t yt-translate-worker .
userStories.notes: Completed in iteration 5. Created runpod/Dockerfile with CUDA 12.4 base, ffmpeg, Python deps, pre-downloaded Whisper medium and Demucs htdemucs models, handler.py entrypoint.

userStories.title: Create requirements-runpod.txt
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a dedicated requirements file for the RunPod worker with all ML dependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: runpod/requirements.txt created with all dependencies
Includes: torch, torchaudio, whisper, demucs, chatterbox-tts, pyannote.audio
Includes: transformers, accelerate (for Qwen3-32B)
Includes: runpod SDK, supabase, httpx
Pinned versions for reproducibility
Typecheck passes after installing deps
userStories.notes: Completed in iteration 6. Created runpod/requirements.txt with all pinned dependencies. Updated Dockerfile to use dedicated requirements file.

userStories.title: Create RunPod client in API gateway
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Railway API gateway to submit jobs to RunPod and poll for results.
acceptanceCriteria: New runpod_client.py module with async functions
submit_job(video_url, target_lang) returns job_id
get_job_status(job_id) returns status dict
cancel_job(job_id) cancels running job
Uses RUNPOD_API_KEY and RUNPOD_ENDPOINT_ID env vars
Proper error handling for API failures
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in iteration 7. Created runpod_client.py with async submit_job, get_job_status, cancel_job functions and RunPodError exception.

userStories.title: Update Railway API to use RunPod
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Railway API endpoints to call RunPod instead of Replicate for video processing.
acceptanceCriteria: /translate endpoint updated to call runpod_client.submit_job()
/status/{job_id} endpoint calls runpod_client.get_job_status()
Job status mapping: RunPod statuses to existing API response format
Backwards compatible response format for frontend
Old Replicate code removed from active endpoints
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in iteration 8. Created server.py with FastAPI gateway, updated Procfile to use server:app.

userStories.title: Create docker-compose for local development
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a docker-compose setup to test the RunPod worker locally with GPU.
acceptanceCriteria: docker-compose.yml created with GPU support
Worker service uses runpod/Dockerfile
Environment variables loaded from .env
Volume mounts for model caching
docker compose up starts worker successfully
Can submit test job via curl and receive result
userStories.notes: Completed in iteration 9. Created docker-compose.yml with GPU support, Dockerfile reference, env_file, volume mounts for models.

userStories.title: Add health check and monitoring
userStories.description: As a developer, I want health checks and basic monitoring in the RunPod worker.
acceptanceCriteria: Handler reports GPU availability on startup
Handler reports loaded models on startup
Failed jobs include detailed error traces
Job duration logged for each completion
Memory usage logged before/after processing
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in iteration 10. Added health check functions (GPU, models, memory), startup logging, job duration tracking, detailed error traces.
